message.content:
Search in D:\service\hse-service\service\src\main\java\com\uae\gov\hse for the incident report DELETE logic. Look for:
1. A controller method handling POST /api/incident/report/delete
2. A service method that performs the deletion of an incident report
3. Any validation that returns "Parameter error" (参数错误) for deletion

Search for keywords: "delete", "incidentReportId", "Parameter error", "参数错误", "deletable", "relatedEmergencyId" in Java files. Report the exact file paths and line numbers where the delete validation logic lives, especially anything that throws/returns code "400" with msg "Parameter error".

attachment.content:
- brainstorming: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.
- frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

## Summary of Incident Report DELETE Logic

I've located all the DELETE validation logic for incident reports in the HSE service. Here are the exact file paths and line numbers:

### 1. Controller - DELETE Endpoint Handler
**File**: `D:\service\hse-service\web\src\main\java\com\uae\gov\hse\web\controller\incidentReport\IncidentReportController.java`

- **Line 86-90**: POST endpoint handler for deletion
  ```
  @PostMapping("/delete")
  public ApiResult<Void> delete(@RequestBody IncidentReportDeleteRequest request) {
      hseIncidentReportService.deleteIncidentReport(request.getIncidentReportId());
      return ApiResult.success(null);
  }
  ```
- **Full API Path**: `/api/incident/report/delete` (POST)
- **Request Class**: `D:\service\hse-service\web\src\main\java\com\uae\gov\hse\web\controller\incidentReport\request\IncidentReportDeleteRequest.java`
  - Parameter: `incidentReportId` (Long)

### 2. Service Interface
**File**: `D:\service\hse-service\service\src\main\java\com\uae\gov\hse\biz\service\incident\HseIncidentReportService.java`

- **Lines 49-52**: Service interface method definition with documentation
  ```
  /**
   * Delete a manually-created OPEN incident report (soft delete).
   * Refuses with BusinessException when the record is auto-created or status != OPEN.
   */
  void deleteIncidentReport(Long incidentReportId);
  ```

### 3. Service Implementation - DELETE Validation Logic
**File**: `D:\service\hse-service\service\src\main\java\com\uae\gov\hse\biz\service\incident\Impl\HseIncidentReportServiceImpl.java`

**Lines 1088-1129**: Complete deletion method with all validations

**Key Validation Checks** (in order):

1. **Line 1089-1090**: Null check on `incidentReportId`
   - Throws: `CommonError.PARAM_ERROR` (Code: 400, Message: "Parameter error")

2. **Line 1092-1094**: Check if incident report exists
   - Throws: `CommonError.PARAM_ERROR` (Code: 400, Message: "Parameter error")

3. **Line 1096-1097**: Check if report is auto-created (has `relatedEmergencyId`)
   - Throws: `IncidentReportEnum.AUTO_CREATED_CANNOT_BE_DELETED` (Code: 81000001, Message: "Auto-created incident reports cannot be deleted")

4. **Line 1099-1100**: Check if status is OPEN
   - Throws: `IncidentReportEnum.ONLY_OPEN_CAN_BE_DELETED` (Code: 81000002, Message: "Only OPEN incident reports can be deleted")

### 4. Error Definitions
**File**: `/d/service/hse-service/common/src/main/java/com/uae/gov/hse/common/exception/error/IncidentReportEnum.java`

- **Line 7**: `PARAM_ERROR(400, "IncidentReport not exist")`
- **Line 9**: `AUTO_CREATED_CANNOT_BE_DELETED(81000001, "Auto-created incident reports cannot be deleted")`
- **Line 10**: `ONLY_OPEN_CAN_BE_DELETED(81000002, "Only OPEN incident reports can be deleted")`

**File**: `/d/service/hse-service/common/src/main/java/com/uae/gov/hse/common/exception/error/CommonError.java`

- **Line 15**: `PARAM_ERROR(400, "Parameter error")`

### Summary Table

| Validation | Line | Check | Error Code | Error Message |
|-----------|------|-------|-----------|--------------|
| Null incidentReportId | 1089-1090 | `incidentReportId == null` | 400 | "Parameter error" |
| Report not found | 1092-1094 | `getById(incidentReportId) == null` | 400 | "Parameter error" |
| Auto-created check | 1096-1097 | `entity.getRelatedEmergencyId() != null` | 81000001 | "Auto-created incident reports cannot be deleted" |
| Status is OPEN | 1099-1100 | `status != OPEN` | 81000002 | "Only OPEN incident reports can be deleted" |
